page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-6xCPU-16GB is a virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture utilizing an AMD EPYC 7542 processor. The configuration provides 6 vCPUs mapped to 6 physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU resources are shared, and the instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 2.67 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data reveals a performance profile that leans toward the lower to middle tiers. Single-core CPU performance under stress-ng is rated in the bottom 10%, while multi-core performance falls into the bottom 25%. However, memory subsystem performance is more resilient, with read-only small block bandwidth reaching 616.67 GB/sec and large block bandwidth at 43.56 GB/sec, both scoring in the average range. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3 operations, also demonstrate average performance. Conversely, static web serving throughput and write-heavy memory operations are weaker, placing in the bottom 25%.

Given the shared CPU allocation and modest compute benchmarks, this server type represents a budget-friendly option for workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated CPU performance. It is well-suited for development environments, lightweight application hosting, background worker processes, and basic database operations that can tolerate shared-resource variability. It is less suitable for high-throughput web serving, intensive LLM inference, or heavy multi-threaded compilation tasks due to its weak multi-core scaling and shared CPU model.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-6xCPU-16GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 6 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 7542 processor and 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 2.0 GHz on an x86_64 architecture, this instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate a budget-oriented profile; single-core and multi-core CPU stress tests register in the lower percentiles, whereas memory read bandwidth and basic compression tasks perform at average levels. Due to its shared CPU allocation, the instance offers a cost-efficient solution for workloads with variable resource demands. It is best suited for general-purpose tasks such as software development, staging environments, lightweight background processing, and moderate database operations, rather than high-throughput web serving or intensive compute workloads.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-6xCPU-16GB is a KVM virtual server featuring 6 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, offering a budget-friendly option for development, staging, and light background workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory (2.67 GB per core)
